lens: EVOLVE @symphony/worker-box-pool into a pure MACHINE pool, and add a thin runtime-adjacent @symphony/dispatch-coordinator that owns the per-run RunSlot sub-lease (MCP tunnel + forwarded port) layered ON TOP of the proven machine lifecycle. The orchestrator stays pure/synchronous and only ever sees a {governs,canAcquire} probe the coordinator now constructs; all async acquire/bind/release lives in runClaim through the coordinator. Single-slot (slotsPerMachine=1) collapses the coordinator to a 1:1 passthrough that reproduces today's acquire->setWorkerHost->settle byte-for-byte (tunnel=null, delegate verbatim), and a mechanical config GATE forbids slotsPerMachine>1 unless the runtime advertises per-run-tunnel capability — so the dossier's central failure mode (a naive max_in_flight bump silently corrupting shared tunnels) is impossible by construction, not by convention.
summary: Today a single BoxRecord fuses two lifecycles: a long-lived MACHINE (boxId/workerHost/providerRef/state/ttl/spend/per-box-mutex) and a per-run LEASE stamped onto it (leaseId, inFlight, leaseIssues, liveLeaseAcquiredMs, affinityKey) at pool.ts stamp() (601-632), unwound at onLeaseSettle() (868-934). maxInFlight>1 already lets one machine carry N leases and the box-second billing is already hardened for it (overlapping liveLeaseAcquiredMs windows + per-box mutex), but the ONLY per-run isolation that does not already work is network: the MCP reverse tunnel is a process-global singleton (worker-host-pool/src/index.ts, `export const workerHostPool`) keyed SOLELY by workerHost with a host-level refCount, and acquireRemoteMcpTunnel/releaseRemoteMcpTunnel (verified at index.ts:21-81) coalesce all runs on a host onto one ssh -N -R child and one remote port. That single fact is why maxInFlight=1 is the only safe value (domain/src/index.ts:191 comment, verified): recycle-out-from-under-a-sibling, last-ref-kill, and localPort-swap-teardown all tear the shared tunnel out from under a sibling run, and the box pool's leaseId/mutex/DESTROYED guards do not extend to it. Critically maxInFlight is defaulted-not-clamped (config.ts:594 `positiveInt(...,1)`, verified) so the safety is by-convention only. Workspaces (remoteWorkspacePath, per issue+slot) and resume-state (workspace-rooted + workerHost-pinned) are ALREADY per-slot safe and are NOT re-solved. THE DESIGN: (step 0) rename BoxRecord->MachineLease INSIDE worker-box-pool as a type alias, keep BoxRecord as a re-export so provider drivers and the green suite stay byte-identical; rename maxInFlight->slotsPerMachine in domain with maxInFlight kept as a deprecated alias. (step 1) introduce @symphony/dispatch-coordinator wrapping the BoxPool as a 1:1 passthrough (tunnel=null) that reproduces acquire->setWorkerHost->settle exactly. (step 2) give each RunSlot its OWN tunnel re-keyed by `${workerHost}#${runKey}` with a DISTINCT remotePort, acquired AFTER lease-bind and closed BEFORE lease-settle on the exactly-once boundary in runClaim's finally. (step 3) add the config-validation GATE that rejects slotsPerMachine>1 unless per-run-tunnel capability is advertised, plus the unconditional workspace/resume slot suffix for same-issue co-residence. (step 4) fix Finding #1 via a narrow BoxPool.swapProvider on reconcile. The orchestrator stays pure/sync (pending:// sentinel KEPT); the coordinator is the only async pool seam beyond the synchronous probe it now constructs. Every step is independently shippable AND reversible because slotsPerMachine defaults to 1.
coordinatorPlacement: NEW package @symphony/dispatch-coordinator: a thin runtime-adjacent facade, NOT a new orchestrator and NOT inside @symphony/worker-box-pool. It depends on @symphony/worker-box-pool (the machine pool) and on an injected TunnelManager port (the per-run MCP/tunnel acquirer); it is depended upon by @symphony/runtime. Three rationales pin the placement: (1) the hard constraint that createBoxPool takes ONLY {clock,logEvent,ledgerPath} and never imports workspace/hook/mcp deps (pool.ts CreateBoxPoolDeps 39-43) means the tunnel/MCP isolation — exactly such a dep — cannot live in worker-box-pool; (2) the orchestrator is pure/synchronous (claim/setWorkerHost/abandonClaim/finish do zero I/O) so the async coordinator cannot live there; (3) the runtime already constructs the pool once and threads it, so the coordinator naturally sits beside it. CONSTRUCTION: a new buildDispatchCoordinator(settings, deps) in apps/cli/src/daemon.ts replaces the buildBoxPool call site — it calls the existing createBoxPool(...) internally with the unchanged {clock,logEvent,ledgerPath} deps, wraps it, and additionally takes the TunnelManager port injected (so worker-box-pool never gains an mcp/tunnel dep, invariant #8 holds). apps/cli/src/main.ts changes from `const boxPool = buildBoxPool(...)` + `new SymphonyRuntime({boxPool})` + `boxPool?.hydrate()` to `const coordinator = buildDispatchCoordinator(...)` + `new SymphonyRuntime({coordinator})` + `coordinator?.hydrate()`; the daemon drain finally (main.ts:163-171) awaits coordinator.drain instead of drainBoxPool, unchanged in shape. HOW IT STAYS OUT OF THE PURE ORCHESTRATOR: the Orchestrator's 4th ctor arg remains the synchronous CapacityProbe {governs,canAcquire} (orchestrator index.ts:57-60,69), now produced by coordinator.capacityProbe() (delegating governs->pool.isEnabled, canAcquire->pool.canAcquire) instead of being hand-built in runtime/src/index.ts:300-302. The orchestrator never sees the coordinator object — only the same synchronous probe shape — so orchestrator.ts is unchanged. All async (acquire machine + bind slot + open tunnel; close tunnel + settle machine) stays in runClaim via the coordinator. The coordinator is itself a thin facade: machine scheduling delegates to the embedded BoxPool byte-for-byte; only RunSlot binding, per-run tunnel isolation, and provider-reconcile are new code.
machineLease: MachineLease IS the long-lived machine identity — exactly what BoxRecord+BoxLease are today once run-state is conceptually lifted off. Lifecycle UNCHANGED, owned by BoxPoolImpl byte-for-byte (these are invariant #4's hardened internals): provision (grow/provisionWarm under reservedProvisions + reservedProvisionsByIssue single-flight, pool.ts 705-787), warm (growTowardTarget toward max(min,warm)), idle (WARM_IDLE + lastIdleAtMs, reaper idleReapMs), ttl (createdAtMs+ttlMs, reaper markedForDestroy), drain (runDrain force-destroy + accrueInFlightWindows + drainEpoch arbitration, 1121-1190), spend/ledger (boxSecondsUsed, daily spend.json with UTC roll, write-ahead ledger). The BoxPool public surface (acquire/canAcquire/isEnabled/reconcile/hydrate/drain/snapshot) is the machine-pool API and stays. STEP-0 RENAME (pure, byte-identical): inside worker-box-pool alias `BoxRecord -> MachineLease` and KEEP `BoxRecord` as a re-export so provider drivers (static-ssh/docker/fly) and the green pool/lease/ledger/mutex/reaper/registry suites keep compiling unchanged. The internal BoxLease (one per stamp; inFlight++, liveLeaseAcquiredMs window; settle inside the per-box mutex; leaseId+settled+DESTROYED-guarded) is RETAINED VERBATIM as the machine-side sub-handle the RunSlot delegates settle to — no new state is added to the record, and leaseIssues/inFlight/liveLeaseAcquiredMs stay where they are because they are genuinely machine-level accounting (box-seconds, fairness). slotsPerMachine is the renamed maxInFlight (config key worker.box_pool.max_in_flight unchanged, still parsed at config.ts:594 positiveInt default 1; domain BoxPoolSettings.maxInFlight 192 gains a slotsPerMachine alias, both defaulting to each other so config.test byte-identical regression holds; comment 191 updated from "the only value safe against the workerHost-keyed MCP tunnel refCount" to "safe for any value once RunSlot owns a per-run tunnel; gated by config validation"). isLeasable/pickRecord/stamp read slotsPerMachine. WHAT OF BoxPool BECOMES THE MACHINE POOL: ALL of it — the inventory Map is the machine registry, boxMutexes is the per-machine lock, reservedProvisions/reservedProvisionsByIssue stay, reaper/reconcileWithProviderList/hydrate/runDrain stay byte-for-byte. SIZED: the machine pool sizes by min/warm/max MACHINES; the coordinator never provisions — it only acquires/binds slots, and machines outlive slots (settle at inFlight->0 returns WARM_IDLE, onLeaseSettle 925-928).
runSlot: RunSlot is the new first-class per-run sub-lease the coordinator mints at dispatch and the runtime releases at run-end; it is owned by the DispatchCoordinator, NOT by the pool. Shape: `interface RunSlot { readonly slotId: string; readonly machineLeaseId: string; readonly issueId: string; readonly slotIndex: number; readonly leaseId: string; readonly workerHost: string; readonly mcpEndpoint: { url: string; remotePort: number } | null; readonly acquiredAtMs: number; heartbeat(): void; release(outcome: BoxOutcome): Promise<void>; fail(reason: string): Promise<void>; }`. BIND AT DISPATCH (coordinator.acquireRunSlot): in strict order (1) `acquired = await pool.acquire(req)` — the existing synchronous select-and-stamp/grow/FIFO-wait path returns a BoxLease over a MachineLease, and on no_capacity/throw returns the SAME typed result the runtime already branches on, so acquire-failure stays one place; (2) `workerHost = acquired.lease.workerHost` — the isolation context is fixed at this moment (its distinct remotePort, its slotIndex-suffixed workspace expectation); (3) only for a remote (SSH-addressable) host, `tunnel = await tunnelManager.openForRun(workerHost, runKey, localHost, localPort)` mints a per-run tunnel with a DISTINCT remotePort (runKey = `${slotIndex}` or leaseId). The RunSlot wraps the BoxLease + the tunnel handle and carries mcpEndpoint.url so acp consumes the resolved URL instead of acquiring its own. If openForRun throws, the coordinator IMMEDIATELY settles the just-acquired BoxLease healthy (so a tunnel fault never leaks a machine lease) and rethrows as the structured acquire error runClaim already handles (index.ts:512-526). RELEASE AT RUN-END (runSlot.release(outcome)/fail(reason)), inside the slot's own idempotent guard, strict order: (1) close THIS slot's tunnel via tunnelManager.closeForRun(workerHost, runKey) — generation-safe because the key embeds runKey, so a stale slot's late close targets its own (now-defunct) key and is a no-op against a newer run's tunnel; (2) delegate to the wrapped BoxLease.release/fail which runs the existing onLeaseSettle inside the per-box mutex (inFlight--, leaseIssues.delete, drop liveLeaseAcquiredMs window, recycle-if-poison-or-markedForDestroy). THE MACHINE OUTLIVES THE SLOT precisely because step (2) only recycles the MachineLease at inFlight==0 (pool.ts 909-929); a sibling RunSlot still holding the machine keeps it LEASED, and crucially its tunnel is a DIFFERENT remotePort untouched by step (1). heartbeat() forwards to BoxLease.heartbeat(). The RunSlot is settled exactly once: it wraps the BoxLease's leaseId+settled+DESTROYED guard and adds its own idempotent flag for the tunnel-close half.

coordinatorApi.name: acquireRunSlot
coordinatorApi.purpose: The SINGLE dispatch entrypoint runClaim calls and the ONE home for acquire-failure. Acquires a MachineLease via pool.acquire (reusing the existing select-and-stamp/grow/FIFO-wait path VERBATIM), then binds a RunSlot: resolves the real workerHost and, for a remote machine, opens a per-run MCP tunnel with a distinct remote port AFTER the lease is bound. Returns the SAME typed no_capacity reasons the pool already returns so the runtime branch is unchanged. A thrown pool fault or a tunnel-open fault surfaces here (the latter after settling the just-acquired lease healthy so no machine lease leaks) and nowhere else.
coordinatorApi.signature: acquireRunSlot(req: { issueId: string; slotIndex: number; labels: ReadonlyArray<string>; affinityKey?: string | null; timeoutMs: number; signal?: AbortSignal; mcp?: { localHost: string; localPort: number } | null }): Promise<{ status: 'bound'; slot: RunSlot } | { status: 'no_capacity'; reason: 'acquire_timeout' | 'spend_cap' | 'pool_disabled' | 'provider_error' }>

coordinatorApi.name: capacityProbe
coordinatorApi.purpose: Returns the synchronous {governs,canAcquire} CapacityProbe handed to the Orchestrator ctor (4th arg). governs() === pool.isEnabled(); canAcquire() === pool.canAcquire(). Structurally identical to today's hand-built probe (runtime index.ts:300-302) so orchestrator.ts is UNCHANGED — only the constructor of the probe moves to the coordinator. Being the same object whose acquireRunSlot is the only acquirer keeps the canAcquire->acquire TOCTOU exactly as today (probe is a hint; acquireRunSlot is authoritative; a lost race returns no_capacity and abandonClaim re-evaluates next tick).
coordinatorApi.signature: capacityProbe(): CapacityProbe | undefined  // CapacityProbe = { governs(): boolean; canAcquire(): boolean }

coordinatorApi.name: isEnabled
coordinatorApi.purpose: Delegates to pool.isEnabled() so runClaim's guard `if (boxPool && boxPool.isEnabled())` (runtime 501) becomes `if (coordinator?.isEnabled())` with identical semantics: a disabled/absent pool falls through to static/local selection byte-for-byte (invariants #9, #10).

coordinatorApi.name: reconcile
coordinatorApi.purpose: Synchronous reload entrypoint called where boxPool.reconcile is called today (runtime index.ts:672-675). First runs the Finding #1 fix (detect a provider-construction change and call pool.swapProvider), THEN delegates to pool.reconcile(next). The coordinator is the natural owner because it already brokers config into the machine layer on reload.

coordinatorApi.name: drain
coordinatorApi.purpose: Delegates to pool.drain; additionally best-effort closes any tunnels for slots whose machines are being force-destroyed past the deadline (there should be none after run-teardown, but drain is the safety net so no ssh -N child outlives the process), closing the recycle-orphans-tunnel gap on shutdown.
coordinatorApi.signature: drain(opts: { deadlineMs: number; signal?: AbortSignal }): Promise<void>

coordinatorApi.name: hydrate
coordinatorApi.purpose: Forwards to pool.hydrate(). The coordinator holds no persistent per-slot state (slots are ephemeral, tunnels process-local) so this is a pure passthrough. Called once at startup (main.ts:127).

coordinatorApi.name: snapshot
coordinatorApi.purpose: Forwards pool.snapshot() (provider/machines/spend, unchanged) and APPENDS the live RunSlot list for TUI/web/history observability of per-slot tunnel ports — the one observability gap once N slots share a machine.
coordinatorApi.signature: snapshot(): BoxPoolSnapshot & { slots: ReadonlyArray<{ slotId: string; machineLeaseId: string; workerHost: string; remotePort: number | null }> }

coordinatorApi.name: swapProvider
coordinatorApi.purpose: Narrow method ON THE POOL (BoxPool.swapProvider), invoked by coordinator.reconcile for Finding #1. Marks old-provider boxes for drain UNDER THE OLD PROVIDER first, then reassigns this.provider = resolveProvider(next.provider, next, deps), re-threads reaperInternals.provider, and updates ledger usesLedger — all WITHOUT reconstructing the singleton. Sequencing discipline (drain-before-swap) prevents destroy() being routed to the new provider for an old box.
coordinatorApi.signature: swapProvider(next: BoxPoolSettings): void  // on BoxPool, called by coordinator.reconcile

orchestratorRuntimeChanges: ORCHESTRATOR (pure/sync, unchanged in spirit): claim/setWorkerHost/abandonClaim/finish stay exactly as in orchestrator/src/index.ts 107-279, doing zero I/O. The pending:// sentinel is KEPT, not dropped — it is the correct synchronous answer to "I have committed a slot but the real host resolves later in async acquire." Dropping it would force host-resolution into the sync poll thread, violating orchestrator purity (invariant #1). claim() still sets `entry.workerHost = pending://${issue.id}/${slotIndex}` synchronously (137) when governs() is true and `entry.affinityHost = retry?.workerHost ?? null` (138); the real host is bound later in runClaim and written back via the SAME orchestrator.setWorkerHost (183-187). The CapacityProbe the orchestrator receives now comes from coordinator.capacityProbe() instead of being hand-built (runtime 300-302), but it is the same {governs,canAcquire} reading pool.isEnabled/pool.canAcquire, so claim() and workerCapacityAvailable() (214-222) are untouched, as is the static least-loaded selectWorkerHost path (governs()===false, invariants #9/#10). CAPACITY/HOST-BIND move INTO the coordinator: capacity is the same probe; host-bind happens in acquireRunSlot which resolves slot.workerHost. RUNTIME runClaim (483-648) changes minimally: the block at 501-541 (`boxPool.acquire` + branch + setWorkerHost) is REPLACED by a single coordinator.acquireRunSlot(...). `lease`/`heartbeatLease` (BoxLease) become `slot`/`heartbeatSlot` (RunSlot). RUNCLAIM BECOMES "acquire a RunSlot, run, release the RunSlot" instead of "acquire a lease, run, settle the lease." ACQUIRE-FAILURE STAYS ONE PLACE: a thrown fault maps to the EXACT box_pool_acquire_error handling (512-526: emit, abandonClaim + handle.release, return — no run/history/lease); a no_capacity reason maps to the EXACT worker_host_capacity dispatch_skipped handling (527-535: abandonClaim + handle.release, return — no retry/backoff). On status 'bound': `effectiveWorkerHost = slot.workerHost` + orchestrator.setWorkerHost(...) (538-540 verbatim, overwriting the sentinel before the runner at 544). heartbeat (550) becomes slot.heartbeat() forwarding to lease.heartbeat(). THE FINALLY (635-647): `await slot.release(boxOutcome)` replaces the direct `lease.fail('box_poisoned')`/`lease.release('healthy')` — slot.release does close-tunnel-THEN-settle-lease in the right order, preserving the exactly-once boundary and the poison/healthy classification (boxOutcome computed at 599 before any early return, structured-prefix-matched POISON_BOX_ERROR_PREFIXES, UNCHANGED). THE REMAINING RUN-TEARDOWN COUPLING is preserved: handle.release() (636) and orchestrator.finish (566 success / 606 failure) stay in runClaim — the coordinator owns ONLY the machine+tunnel resources, not the run's history/handle, and release still fires exactly once in the finally alongside them. The MCP-tunnel acquire that today happens INSIDE the runner (acp startSession -> acquireAgentMcpEndpoint -> workerHostPool.acquireRemoteMcpTunnel, verified at agentEndpoint.ts:106) MOVES UP into acquireRunSlot; acp.startSession stops acquiring and instead receives slot.mcpEndpoint.url threaded through runAgentAttempt input (the per-run token issue/revoke in agentEndpoint stays). RISK CARRIED: an acp session restart/resume within one run MUST reuse slot.mcpEndpoint.url, not re-acquire. The CapacityProbe is installed once for the orchestrator lifetime (not reinstalled on reload); governs/canAcquire simply re-read swapped settings.
shardingIsolation: THE BLOCKER (verified at worker-host-pool/src/index.ts:16-103): WorkerHostPool keys remoteMcpTunnels as Map<workerHost, RemoteMcpTunnelEntry>, acquireRemoteMcpTunnel(workerHost, localHost, localPort) refcounts ONE shared entry per host (refCount += 1, returns the SAME remotePort; index.ts:26-35) else mints one remotePort per host (nextRemoteMcpPort/availableRemoteMcpPorts from 46000) and starts ONE `ssh -N -R remotePort:localHost:localPort` child; releaseRemoteMcpTunnel(workerHost) decrements and kills the child only at the last ref (index.ts:73-81); a localHost/localPort mismatch closes the LIVE shared entry (index.ts:36). With N slots on one machine this yields: (mode 1) recycle-out-from-under-a-sibling — box pool destroys the machine on inFlight==0 with NO releaseRemoteMcpTunnel call; (mode 2) last-ref-kill — the first finisher kills the shared tunnel under a still-running sibling; (mode 3) localPort-swap-on-reload tears down the live entry; (mode 4) recycled-port collision. THE PLAN — PER-RUN TUNNEL, DISTINCT PORTS (the shared sharding core, kept verbatim across both designs): introduce a TunnelManager port owned by the coordinator with openForRun(workerHost, runKey, localHost, localPort) / closeForRun(workerHost, runKey) where runKey = `${slotIndex}` (or leaseId). Re-key remoteMcpTunnels from Map<workerHost> to Map<`${workerHost}#${runKey}`> and STOP coalescing by host — the existing nextRemoteMcpPort/availableRemoteMcpPorts allocator already mints DISTINCT ports, it just needs to stop collapsing them, so each RunSlot gets its OWN reverse tunnel + remote port. This eliminates the shared refcount for pooled runs: mode 1 — recycling the machine for run A no longer affects run B, which holds its own (host,B.runKey) tunnel the coordinator closes only on B's release; mode 2 — no last-ref kill, each slot closes only its own child; mode 3 — a localPort swap only affects the slot acquiring with the new port, no shared entry to mismatch; mode 4 — guarded by a port-exhaustion ceiling. ORDERING (load-bearing): the coordinator opens the tunnel in acquireRunSlot AFTER lease-bind and closes it in slot.release BEFORE settling the lease — so machine recycle can NEVER run while a tunnel is live (the lease is not settled until the tunnel is closed), closing the recycle-orphans-tunnel gap; and generation-safety rides the runKey in the map so a stale late close is a no-op. acquireAgentMcpEndpoint moves behind the coordinator (acp consumes slot.mcpEndpoint.url; the host-keyed methods are KEPT for the local/non-pool/single-tenant fallback rather than deleted). WORKSPACE/RESUME isolation is ALREADY correct (remoteWorkspacePath keys on workerHost+identifier+slot; resume-state is workspace-rooted + workerHost-pinned) and is NOT re-solved, with ONE sharp edge FORCED: when ensembleSize==1 both workspacePath and remoteWorkspacePath return the bare <root>/<identifier> with no slot suffix (verified semantics), which would clobber if two slots of one issue co-reside. So when two slots of one issue MAY co-reside (a maxBoxesPerIssue/slotsPerMachine interaction), the coordinator FORCES an UNCONDITIONAL slotIndex suffix on the workspace/resume path — STRICTLY GATED behind a co-residence flag so it never alters the existing ensembleSize==1 single-slot path layout that resume-state and live-ssh tests depend on. SLOTSPERMACHINE SIZING: default stays 1 (single tunnel per machine == today, single-tenant path byte-for-byte). The local-host path (workerHost null) needs no tunnel and is unaffected.
migration: EVOLVE not rewrite — every step keeps the suite green and is independently shippable AND reversible (run `pnpm check`/`pnpm tidy`, NOT mise, per MEMORY: mise fails on date-pinned tool installs in this sandbox). STEP 0 (pure rename INSIDE worker-box-pool, decoupled from the coordinator; lowest-risk first commit; suite byte-identical before any new package exists): alias `BoxRecord -> MachineLease` and KEEP `BoxRecord` as a re-export so provider drivers and the green pool/lease/ledger/mutex/reaper/registry suites + box-pool-wiring + box-pool-e2e-memory keep compiling unchanged; rename `maxInFlight -> slotsPerMachine` in domain with `maxInFlight` kept as a deprecated alias parsed by config.ts:594 (max_in_flight key unchanged); isLeasable/pickRecord/stamp read slotsPerMachine. No behavior change; the config byte-identical regression (absent box_pool => deep-equal clone) holds because absent => default 1 => same object. STEP 1 (coordinator as 1:1 PASSTHROUGH): create @symphony/dispatch-coordinator wrapping BoxPool; acquireRunSlot calls pool.acquire and wraps the BoxLease in a RunSlot whose tunnel is null (no remote tunnel change yet, acp still acquires the old way); capacityProbe/isEnabled/reconcile/hydrate/drain/snapshot delegate VERBATIM. Wire buildDispatchCoordinator in daemon.ts and swap boxPool->coordinator in main.ts + SymphonyRuntime; replace runClaim's 501-541 acquire block and 635-647 finally with coordinator.acquireRunSlot/slot.release. Because the passthrough reproduces acquire->setWorkerHost->settle EXACTLY (tunnel=null, delegate verbatim), runtime.test.ts and orchestrator.test.ts stay green and the absent-pool path stays byte-for-byte (coordinator undefined == boxPool undefined today). STEP 2 (per-run tunnel): add the TunnelManager port, re-key worker-host-pool by `${workerHost}#${runKey}` ALONGSIDE the existing host-keyed methods (KEEP them for local/non-pool/single-tenant fallback), route acp's mcp endpoint through slot.mcpEndpoint.url. Single-slot still allocates exactly one tunnel per machine so live-ssh/e2e behavior is unchanged; ADD a test asserting two slots on one host get distinct remotePorts and that closeForRun(A) leaves B's tunnel alive. STEP 3 (unlock slotsPerMachine>1 behind the GATE): config validation now PERMITS >1 only when the runtime advertises per-run-tunnel capability; FORCE the unconditional slot suffix for same-issue co-residence (gated by the co-residence flag); add props tests for N-per-machine billing (already covered by liveLeaseAcquiredMs) + tunnel isolation + port-exhaustion ceiling. STEP 4 (Finding #1): reconcile rebuilds-and-swaps the provider in place via the narrow BoxPool.swapProvider; add a test that a reconcile changing provider rebuilds it and that same-provider reconcile skips the swap; cover an in-flight lease during a provider change. BACKWARD-COMPAT: slotsPerMachine default 1 makes the coordinator a 1:1 passthrough byte-identical to today at every step.
subsumes: FINDING #1 (provider not rebuilt on reload — verified: pool.ts:161 resolves `this.provider = resolveProvider(...)` once in the ctor, stored `private readonly provider`, and reconcile swaps settings but never re-runs resolveProvider) is SUBSUMED because the coordinator OWNS the reconcile broker seam (coordinator.reconcile -> pool.swapProvider then pool.reconcile). Grafting Design 2's explicit reconcileProvider/swapProvider decomposition: a NARROW BoxPool.swapProvider(next) that (1) marks old-provider boxes for drain UNDER THE OLD PROVIDER first — articulating Design 2's sequencing hazard precisely (destroy() must not be routed to the NEW provider for an OLD box), (2) reassigns this.provider = resolveProvider(next.provider, next, deps), (3) re-threads reaperInternals.provider, and (4) updates the ledger usesLedger gate — all WITHOUT reconstructing the singleton (the hard invariant from dossier §7). This eliminates the books-vs-backend divergence (new settings everywhere but old provider provisioning/destroying) and treats #1 as a deliberate, tested change rather than the current silent pin. FINDING #3 (per-issue refcount over slots) is SUBSUMED by making RunSlot first-class. Today the per-issue cap is approximated by leaseIssues: Set<string> on the record + reservedProvisionsByIssue (pool.ts issueWouldExceedCap 635-646), counting MACHINES an issue touches, not RUN SLOTS. Once RunSlot exists the coordinator holds the authoritative per-(issueId,slotIndex,leaseId) registry, so per-issue fairness becomes a precise refcount of live RunSlots per issue rather than a per-machine Set-membership approximation; maxBoxesPerIssue stays as the MACHINE-level cap and an optional per-issue slot cap rides the same RunSlot registry — the slot abstraction is exactly the missing accounting unit #3 asked for, with no change to the hardened machine-level reservation single-flight. The two findings are also joined at the slot layer: the ensembleSize==1 bare-workspace edge #3 worries about (two slots of one issue clobbering the shared workspace/resume path) is closed by the SAME sharding plan that forces an unconditional slot suffix when two slots of one issue may co-reside.
risks: The pending:// sentinel is KEPT, not dropped — host resolves in async acquire, so dropping it would break orchestrator sync purity. Every existing sentinel-tolerant reader (cleanupWorkerHost mapping pending://->null so reconcile cleanup stays LOCAL, snapshot passthrough surfacing the literal pending:// rather than a misleading null, finish->RetryEntry affinity) MUST remain; this is a conscious non-removal, not an omission. Any new per-run host identity must not re-introduce SSH to a sentinel host.

Re-keying worker-host-pool from Map<workerHost> to Map<workerHost#runKey> changes a process-global singleton consumed by acp/mcp directly (agentEndpoint.ts:106 acquire, the release path keys on workerHost alone). If any caller still releases by workerHost alone it will leak or mis-target — acp's MCP endpoint MUST be routed through the RunSlot in the same step (step 2) or the refcount invariant breaks. Keep the host-keyed methods for local/non-pool fallback but prefer collapsing to always-slot-keyed once step 2 proves the single-slot path stays byte-identical, to avoid a long-lived dual path drifting.

ssh -N child + remote-port fan-out grows from 1/host to N/host (starting 46000): per-slot tunnels multiply both ssh processes (fd/process pressure on a busy controller) and port consumption. availableRemoteMcpPorts recycles, but a port-exhaustion ceiling/error MUST be added before defaulting slotsPerMachine>1, and a recycled-port-vs-stale-entry collision (the existing exited/closeRemoteMcpTunnel logic assumed one entry per host) must be guarded. Load-test before raising the default.

An acp session restart/resume within one run MUST reuse slot.mcpEndpoint.url rather than re-acquire, now that the tunnel acquire moved up from acp.startSession into coordinator.acquireRunSlot and the tunnel lifetime spans the whole RunSlot rather than the agent session. Verify acp's session-restart path threads slot.mcpEndpoint.url through, not a fresh acquire.

swapProvider on reconcile is the trickiest new behavior: swapping this.provider while OLD-provider boxes are still live risks destroy()/list()/probe() being routed to the NEW provider for an OLD box, orphaning paid cloud boxes. Must mark old-provider boxes for drain UNDER THE OLD PROVIDER before swapping (honoring 'drain never leaks a paid cloud box'), never reconstruct the singleton, and the test MUST cover an in-flight lease during a provider change.

Forcing an unconditional slot suffix on workspace/resume paths for same-issue co-residence changes path layout for a case that currently can't happen; it MUST be gated strictly behind a 'two slots of one issue may co-reside' flag so it never alters the ensembleSize==1 single-slot path layout that resume-state and live-ssh tests depend on. The slotIndex tunnel-isolation key and this workspace-suffix fix share the same uniqueness assumption — confirm the coordinator never places two slots with the same (issueId, slotIndex) on one host — and must be validated together.

slotsPerMachine>1 multiplies the blast radius of a poisoned machine across N concurrent runs (one bad box recycles N runs on inFlight->0). The per-run tunnel isolation prevents cross-run tunnel kills but NOT this shared-machine-poison correlation; it is inherent to sharing a machine and must be an explicit operator tradeoff, defaulting to 1.

The config-validation GATE (reject slotsPerMachine>1 unless per-run-tunnel capability is advertised) is the single mechanical safety; if the capability advertisement is mis-wired (advertised when the per-run tunnel is not actually active, e.g. the host-keyed fallback path is still live), the gate would pass a corrupting config. The capability flag must be derived from the TunnelManager actually being per-run-keyed, not a static config bit.
